summary:
Routine nasobiliary drainage is not needed after successful endoscopic clearance of the common bile duct in choledocholithiasis-induced cholangitis, as modern ERCP provides immediate and verifiable biliary decompression via direct stone retrieval and imaging confirmation. The absence of postprocedural benefit supports its non-routine use.  

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{B}}$
